errors.noRewardAddresses: No reward addresses found. Please ensure your wallet has a stake key.

updateDID.title: Update DID
updateDID.description: Update your DID Document with new information or service endpoints.

errors.didNotFound: DID not found. Cannot update a DID that does not exist.
errors.walletMismatch: Wallet stake address does not match DID.

whatIsDID.a: A DID (Decentralized Identifier) is a digital ID that you fully own and control — no company or government can take it away. Think of it like a username that lives on the blockchain.

whyNeedOne.a: A DID lets you prove your identity online without relying on centralized services. It can be used for signing documents, logging into apps, or proving ownership of credentials.

howWallet.a: A Cardano wallet (like Eternl, Nami, or Lace) is a browser extension that stores your blockchain keys. You'll need one to create and manage your DID. It's free to install.

howCreate.q: How do I create a DID?
howCreate.a: Connect your wallet, go to the Create tab, and click "Create DID". The app will generate a DID document, store it on IPFS (decentralized storage), and record it on the Cardano blockchain. You'll be asked to sign a transaction.

howUpdate.a: Yes. The Update tab lets you change your DID document — for example, adding a service endpoint (a URL where others can reach you). Each update creates a new version on-chain.

howRevoke.a: Revoking permanently deactivates your DID. Do this only if your keys are compromised or you no longer need the identity. This action cannot be undone.

revokeDID.title: Revoke DID
revokeDID.description: Permanently revoke a DID. This cannot be undone.

errors.didNotFound: DID not found. Cannot revoke a DID that does not exist.
errors.walletMismatch: Wallet stake address does not match DID.

revokeDID.revokedWarning: This DID can no longer be updated or used for authentication.
revokeDID.confirmQuestion: Are you sure you want to revoke this DID?
revokeDID.confirmWarning: This action is permanent and cannot be undone. Your DID will be permanently deactivated. Since your DID is tied to your wallet's stake address, you will need a completely new wallet to create a new DID.
